LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF GARY JARUMAY, ALAN JARUMAY, AND MARY FLORES' MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE AND TRANSFER IS GRANTED. THE CONSOLIDATION IS FOR ALL PURPOSES. THE CASE MANAGEMENT DATE REMAINS. PLAINTIFF TO PAY TRANSFER COSTS. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING. PROPOSED ORDER TO INDICATE THERE IS NO OPPOSITION. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MTORREANO@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-02-2020 Vacated

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Jul-02-2020 Text Ruling

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-25-2019 Continued To Aug-24-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-22-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Indrajana, Esq. 650-597-0928 of Indrajana Law Group for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. Plaintiff informs the Court the motion is premature. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LILY GROVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Lily Grove's motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendant fails to sustain her summary judgment burden. First, claim/issue preclusion does not apply to this case. The probate proceeding did not decide whether decedent was the victim of fraud or elder abuse. (See Estate of Dito (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 791, 802, 805 [petition asserting claim of financial elder abuse was not barred by res judicata where it raised a different primary right from that considered in the prior probate proceeding].) Further, Defendant does not address Plaintiffs' allegation in the first amended complaint, or their factual showing, that much of the conduct to which they object was unknown to them at the time of the probate proceedings because of Defendant's alleged concealment of facts, and therefore was not and could not have the subject of objections filed in that proceeding. (FAC 30, 31, 36, 37; Jarumay Decl. 8-9, 11, 12.) (See Teselle v. McLoughlin (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 156, 173-176 [defendants' motion for summary adjudication of elder financial abuse claim was insufficient where it failed to refute a tenable pleaded theory and did not conclusively negate an element of the cause of action].) Second, Defendant's "factually devoid" argument fails as Defendant does not specifically highlight any factually devoid discovery responses in compliance with California Rule of Court 3.1350(h). Rather, Defendant generally cites to Plaintiff's discovery responses. (See Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts 15, 16, 17, 18, 21, 22, 24.) The procedural deficiencies in Defendant's moving papers render them insufficient to meet her burden.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief
Off Calendar Dec-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Sanjiv N. Singh, Esq., SNS PLC, Telephone 415-8165542, appearing for Plaintiffs
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LILY GROVE'S Motion And Motion For Relief GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. On 9/27/28 Plaintiff Mary Ann Flores served Request for Production, Set One by placing the documents under the locked door, during regular business hours at the law office of Denise Wright, counsel for Defendant LILY GROVE. On 10/31/18 responses, containing all objections were served. No extension was obtained by Ms. Wright and the responses were several days late. Defendant LILY GROVE'S motion is GRANTED as to Request for Production No. 15 and her objection is preserved. Federal and state tax returns are privileged and cannot be discovered in litigation, except under very limited circumstances such as voluntary waiver. Webb v. Standard Oil Co. (1957) 49 Cal.2d 509, 512-513; Schnabel v. Superior Court (1993) 5 Cal.4th 704. Prevailing party to prepare and submit order which repeats verbatim the Courts tentative ruling by email to Judge Pro Tem Peter Van Zandt at peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-28-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Sanjiv N. Singh, Esq., SNS PLC, Telephone 415-8165542, appearing for Plaintiffs
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant LILY GROVE'S "Motion for Protective Order and Sanctions" is DENIED. The Subpoena for financial records was issued in the consolidated case in which Defendant LILY GROVES had appeared pursuant to Hamilton v. Asbestos Ltd. (2000) 22 Cal.4th 1127, 1148-1149 and CCP Section 2031.020. The Court finds that Plaintiffs complied with CCP Section 1985.3(1)(b) and proper notice to consumer was attempted on Defendant LILY GROVE. In addition, on or around July 5, 2018, Defendant LILY GROVE received letters from financial institutions providing notice that those records were being sought. The appropriate course of action would have been to bring a Motion to Quash which does not appear in the record. Prevailing party to prepare and submit order which repeats verbatim the Courts tentative ruling by email to Judge Pro Tem Peter Van Zandt at peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Consolidate And Transfer.
Off Calendar Jan-23-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Indrajana, Esq. 650-597-0928 of Indrjana Law Group for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court inquires why the other side is not present. Plaintiff reports the status of the case. The Court calls Ms. Dennis Right, Counsel for Lily Grove on the San Mateo case on speaker phone. The receptionist notifies the Court Ms. Right is on trial in Monterey. The Court advises the receptionist the granting of the motion, transferring the San Mateo case to San Francisco and the cases are consolidated for all purposes. The transfer costs are to be paid by Plaintiff Mr. Jarumay.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF GARY JARUMAY, ALAN JARUMAY, MARY FLORES' Motion To Consolidate And Transfer is Granted. The consolidation is for all purposes. The case management date remains. Plaintiff to pay transfer costs. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Counsel for Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling. Proposed order to indicate there is no Opposition.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, mtorreano@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)
